key_insight: A graph-based spatio-temporal model using longitudinal MRI scans can better predict individualized breast cancer response to neoadjuvant chemotherapy than standard imaging baselines.
clinical_relevance: If validated, this approach could help identify which patients are more likely to achieve pathological complete response and support more tailored treatment planning.
method: Models each patient’s MRI timepoints as a relational graph over time.
Uses a state-of-the-art graph neural network to learn treatment trajectory patterns.
Adds three self-supervised objectives to improve representation learning from limited labeled data.
evidence_signal: In a cohort of 585 ISPY-2 breast cancer patients, the method outperformed vision and self-supervised baselines across multiple classification metrics.
watch_item: Performance was shown on a public retrospective dataset, so external validation and real-world prospective testing are still needed before broader use.
paper_title: Graph Representation Learning of Longitudinal Medical Imaging Trajectories for Treatment Response Prediction
